title: Automates text extraction, transcription and summarization of audio, video, and
summary: This repo understands how to compose a multimedia processing pipeline from AWS managed services.  Its key insight is the Chain of Responsibility + Factory pattern for dynamic handler discovery — readers, processors, and writers snap together like pipeline stages.
creator_view: This repo understands how to compose a multimedia processing pipeline from AWS managed services. Its key insight is the Chain of Responsibility + Factory pattern for dynamic handler discovery — readers, processors, and writers snap together like pipeline stages. It demonstrates PII anonymization round-tripping (tokenize before Bedrock, untokenize after), multi-format extraction (YouTube, PDF, Excel, Word, Quip, HTTP, S3), and prompt templating for Bedrock summarization. The architecture is more interesting than the code — it's a pattern reference, not production software.

repo.description: Automates text extraction, transcription and summarization of audio, video, and documents using Amazon Bedrock, Transcribe, Textract and other AWS services. Supports summarization and interactive chat.

QS-01_activity_recency.detail: Last push 2024-09-27 (~18 months ago). 48 total commits. Stale.

QS-03_archived_abandoned.detail: Not archived but no commits in 18 months. Functionally dormant.

QS-04_license.detail: No license file. Cannot legally reuse.

QS-05_cicd.detail: Zero workflow runs. No CI/CD configured.

QS-08_code_scanning.detail: No CodeQL or code scanning configured.

QS-16_known_cves.detail: Not checked via deps.dev (Python ecosystem, no npm).

QS-17_fork_to_star.detail: 5/18 = 0.28. Moderate fork ratio for demo repo.

security.detail: No scanning, no dependabot, no branch protection, no OpenSSF.

reliability.detail: No CI/CD, no tests visible, single contributor.

maintainability.detail: Clean Chain of Responsibility + Factory pattern. Well-organized handlers.

documentation.detail: Thorough README with architecture overview, but no other docs. No license.

process.detail: No CI, no protection, no contribution guidelines, no code review evidence.

velocity.detail: Dormant 18 months. 48 commits total over 8 months of active dev.

absenceDetail: Solo developer demo project. Missing: license, CI/CD, tests, security scanning, branch protection, contribution guidelines, multiple contributors. Consistent with proof-of-concept intended to demonstrate AWS service integration.

Its key insight is the Chain of Responsibility + Factory pattern for dynamic handler discovery — readers, processors, and writers snap together like pipeline stages. It demonstrates PII anonymization round-tripping (tokenize before Bedrock, untokenize after), multi-format extraction (YouTube, PDF, Excel, Word, Quip, HTTP, S3), and prompt templating for Bedrock summarization. The architecture is more interesting than the code — it's a pattern reference, not production software.

adoptionPartial.WR-01_stack_compatibility: Low — Python/AWS, not TypeScript/Firebase. No overlap with home stack.
adoptionPartial.WR-04_lock_in: High — deeply coupled to 5+ AWS services (Bedrock, Transcribe, Textract, Comprehend, S3).
